items.q: What is the main advantage of asymmetric encryption?
items.correct: Secure key exchange without sharing a secret in advance.
items.misconception: It is faster than symmetric encryption.
items.why: Students think asymmetric is just 'better'. It's actually SLOWER than symmetric. Its advantage is solving key distribution - the public key can be shared openly without compromising security.

items.q: What is the worst-case complexity of bubble sort?
items.misconception: O(n) because it just passes through the list.
items.why: Students count one pass and forget that bubble sort does up to n passes, each comparing up to n elements. The nested loop structure gives n times n = n squared.

items.q: Why is UDP used for live video streaming despite being unreliable?
items.correct: Lower overhead and latency - retransmitting late packets would harm real-time quality.
items.misconception: Because TCP cannot handle video data.
items.random_wrong: Because UDP is always faster than TCP.
items.why: Students assume reliability is always desirable. For real-time media, a dropped packet is preferable to a delayed one - UDP trades reliability for timeliness, which suits live media.

items.q: Which is a high-level programming language?
items.why: Correct answer: Python. “Assembly” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: Integrated Development Environment. “Internet Data Engine” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In Python, how many spaces are conventionally used for one level of indentation (PEP 8)?
items.why: The real figure is 4 spaces. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
